title: Thunder's Gilgeous-Alexander Controls Series vs Spurs
body:
As the Western Conference Finals heat up, the Oklahoma City Thunder and San Antonio Spurs are set to face off in a crucial Game 4 matchup. The series has been a thrilling ride so far, with the Thunder's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander emerging as a dominant force, controlling the pace of the games and leaving the Spurs scrambling to keep up. The Spurs, on the other hand, are fighting to stay afloat, particularly in the minutes when their highly touted young star, Victor Wembanyama, is not on the court. This unexpected dynamic has turned the series on its head, with the Thunder pushing the Spurs to make significant adjustments just to keep pace.

One of the most surprising aspects of this series has been the Spurs' inability to dictate the terms of the game, even with Wembanyama on the court. The young phenom was expected to be the catalyst for the Spurs' success, but instead, it's been Gilgeous-Alexander who has been calling the shots. The Thunder's star point guard has been virtually unstoppable, using his quickness and agility to blow past defenders and get to the rim at will. The Spurs have tried various defensive schemes to slow him down, but so far, nothing has seemed to work. As a result, the Thunder have been able to build significant leads, putting the Spurs in a difficult position as they try to mount a comeback.

The Spurs' struggles in the non-Wembanyama minutes have been particularly glaring. Without their young star on the court, the team seems to lack a sense of direction and purpose, struggling to score and defend with any consistency. This has put a tremendous amount of pressure on Wembanyama to perform when he is on the court, and while he has shown flashes of brilliance, it's clear that he can't do it alone. The Spurs need to find a way to be more effective in these stretches if they hope to get back in the series. This could involve making some significant adjustments to their lineup or rotation, or finding ways to get more production from their role players.

Despite the challenges they face, the Spurs are not out of this series yet. They have shown glimpses of the form that made them a formidable opponent in the regular season, and if they can find a way to shore up their defense and get more consistent production from their supporting cast, they may still be able to push the Thunder to the limit. However, this will require a significant improvement in their overall performance, particularly on the defensive end. The Thunder have been able to score at will, and if the Spurs can't find a way to slow them down, it's unlikely they'll be able to get back in the series.

As the series heads into its pivotal fourth game, the stakes are high for both teams. The Thunder are looking to take a commanding 3-1 lead and put the Spurs on the brink of elimination, while the Spurs are desperate to get back in the series and avoid falling into a deep hole. The matchup promises to be an intense and closely contested one, with both teams leaving everything on the court. In the end, it will come down to which team can make the necessary adjustments and find a way to outexecute their opponent.

Looking ahead to Game 4, it's clear that the Thunder have the momentum and the confidence. However, the Spurs are a proud and resilient team, and they will not go down without a fight. As the series continues to unfold, it will be fascinating to see how these two teams respond to the challenges they face, and which one will ultimately emerge victorious. One thing is certain, though: the Western Conference Finals have been full of surprises so far, and there's no reason to think that will change anytime soon.
